chapter.label: Tale 121
chapter.title: The Life And Death Of Gregory,

paragraphs.text: THE BYZANTINE, AND OF ANOTHER GREGORY, HIS DISCIPLE, FROM PARAN

[The fathers of the same place told us concerning Abba Gregory of Byzantium and his disciple, Abba Gregory from Paras, that they stayed on an island in the Red Sea. Now there was no water on that istand: they used to bring the water to supply their needs from the mainland. They had a boat in which they would set out and bring back water. Onc day they put the boat in the water and tied it to a stone. That night a great wave came and broke the rope, sending the boat to the bottom. Now the fathers were left with no way of providing themselves with water. Eight months Jater, some monks came from Raithou and found iliem both dead. They also found a tortoise-shell with this written on it: ‘Abba Gregory of Pharon died after going twenty-eight days without drinking water, bul I have gone thirty-seven days without a drink". We found both the corpses intact: we took them back and buried them at Raithou.]
